heading: Installation and removal of electrical facilities
label: 504 Installation and removal of electrical facilities

L. 90–251, § 482 Stat. 4Jan. 30, 1968504(b)36:725 (3d sentence).504(c)36:725 (last sentence).In subsection (a), the words “lighting or other” and “for illumination or other purposes” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (c), the words “illumination or other” are omitted as unnecessary. children.heading: .—Installation
children.label: a .—Installation
children.text: The Mayor of the District of Columbia may allow the Inaugural Committee to install suitable overhead conductors and electrical facilities, with adequate supports. The official in charge of a park or reservation in the District of Columbia in which it is necessary to place wires shall supervise the placing and removal of those wires.

children.heading: .—Removal
children.label: b .—Removal
children.text: The conductors and supports shall be removed not later than 5 days after the end of the inaugural period.

children.heading: .—Indemnification
children.label: c .—Indemnification
children.text: The United States Government and the District of Columbia may not incur any expense or damage from the installation, operation, or removal of a temporary overhead conductor or electrical facility. The Inaugural Committee shall indemnify and hold harmless the District of Columbia and the appropriate department, agency, or instrumentality of the Government against any loss or damage, and against any liability arising, from any act of the Inaugural Committee or any agent, licensee, servant, or employee of the Inaugural Committee in connection with the installation, operation, or removal of a temporary overhead conductor or electrical facility.
